subject: Ancient History
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The last Mauryan ruler, Brihadratha was assassinated by his commander-in-chief, Pushyamitra Sunga. 2. The last Sunga king, Devabhuti was assassinated by his Brahmana Minister Vasudeva Kanva who usurped the throne. 3. The last ruler of the Kanva dynasty was deposed by the Andhras. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail: The Maurya (321 BCE – 185 BCE), Sunga (185 BCE – 73 BCE), and Kanva dynasties (73 BCE – 30 BCE) represent significant eras in ancient Indian history, spanning the rule from centralized imperial administration to regional governance and eventual fragmentation. Statement 1 is correct: The last Mauryan ruler, Brihadratha, was assassinated by his commander-in-chief, Pushyamitra Sunga, who then established the Sunga dynasty (185 BCE). Statement 2 is correct: The last Sunga king, Devabhuti, was assassinated by his minister Vasudeva Kanva, a Brahmana, who subsequently usurped the throne and founded the Kanva dynasty (73 BCE). Statement 3 is correct: The last ruler of the Kanva dynasty was overthrown by the Andhras (also known as the Satavahanas) around 30 BCE, marking the end of the Kanva dynasty and the establishment of the Satavahana dynasty in central India.

statements.text: The last Mauryan ruler, Brihadratha, was assassinated by his commander-in-chief, Pushyamitra Sunga, who then established the Sunga dynasty (185 BCE).
The last Sunga king, Devabhuti, was assassinated by his minister Vasudeva Kanva, a Brahmana, who subsequently usurped the throne and founded the Kanva dynasty (73 BCE).

The last ruler of the Kanva dynasty was overthrown by the Andhras (also known as the Satavahanas) around 30 BCE, marking the end of the Kanva dynasty and the establishment of the Satavahana dynasty in central India.

subject: Ancient History
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Vardhaman Mahavira’s mother was the daughter of Lichchhavi chief Chetaka 2. Gautama Buddha’s mother was a princess from the Koshalan dynasty 3. Parshvanatha, the twenty-third Tirthankara, belonged to Benaras. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 2 and 3
solution.detail: Buddhism and Jainism part of the Shramana traditions are two ancient Indian religions that originated around the same time, during the 6th century BCE. They emphasized asceticism, non-violence, and liberation from the cycle of rebirth. They offered alternative philosophies to Vedic ritualism, focusing on personal spiritual practices and ethical living. Statement 1 is incorrect: Vardhaman Mahavira’s mother, Trishala, was the sister (and not daughter) of Chetaka, a chief of the Lichchhavi clan. The Lichchhavis were known for their democratic and republican system of governance. Statement 2 is correct: Gautama Buddha’s mother, Maya Devi, was a princess from the Koshalan dynasty. She was married to King Suddhodana of the Shakya clan and was the mother of Siddhartha Gautama, who later became the Buddha. Statement 3 is correct: Parshvanatha, the twenty-third Tirthankara of Jainism, was associated with Benaras (Varanasi), where he was born. According to Jain tradition, he was born into a royal family and preached a doctrine of nonviolence, truth, and asceticism. The serpent symbol is associated with him. 31. Assertion (A): Ashoka annexed Kalinga to the Mauryan Empire. (2000) Reason (R): Kalinga controlled the land and sea routes to South India. (a) Both A and R are true, and R is the correct explanation of A (b) Both A and R are true, but R is not a correct explanation of A (c) A is true, but R is false (d) A is false, but R is true

statements.text: Vardhaman Mahavira’s mother, Trishala, was the sister (and not daughter) of Chetaka, a chief of the Lichchhavi clan. The Lichchhavis were known for their democratic and republican system of governance.

Gautama Buddha’s mother, Maya Devi, was a princess from the Koshalan dynasty. She was married to King Suddhodana of the Shakya clan and was the mother of Siddhartha Gautama, who later became the Buddha.

subject: Ancient History
stem: Emperor Harsha’s southward march was stopped on the Narmada river by:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Pulakesin-II
solution.detail: Option (a) is incorrect: Pulakeshin-I was the founder of the Chalukya dynasty and ruled in the early 6th century, i.e. before Harsha’s time. Option (b) is correct: Emperor Harsha (606–647 CE) expanded his empire across North India, ruling from Kannauj and influencing regions like Punjab, Bengal, Odisha, and Central India. Seeking to extend his control southward beyond the Vindhyas, he invaded the Deccan but was defeated by Pulakeshin II, the Chalukya ruler (c. 610–642 CE), around 618–619 CE at the Narmada River. This defeat established the Narmada as the boundary between Harsha’s northern empire and the Chalukyan kingdom, preventing further expansion into the south. Option (c) is incorrect: Vikramaditya-I was a later Chalukya king, ruling after Pulakeshin-II. Option (d) is incorrect: Vikramaditya-II ruled even later than Vikramaditya-I and his reign did not overlap with Harsha’s.

optionNotes.text: Pulakeshin-I was the founder of the Chalukya dynasty and ruled in the early 6th century, i.e. before Harsha’s time.

Emperor Harsha (606–647 CE) expanded his empire across North India, ruling from Kannauj and influencing regions like Punjab, Bengal, Odisha, and Central India. Seeking to extend his control southward beyond the Vindhyas, he invaded the Deccan but was defeated by Pulakeshin II, the Chalukya ruler (c. 610–642 CE), around 618–619 CE at the Narmada River. This defeat established the Narmada as the boundary between Harsha’s northern empire and the Chalukyan kingdom, preventing further expansion into the south.
Vikramaditya-I was a later Chalukya king, ruling after Pulakeshin-II.
Vikramaditya-II ruled even later than Vikramaditya-I and his reign did not overlap with Harsha’s.

subject: Ancient History
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The Cholas defeated Pandya and Chera rulers and established their domination over peninsular India in the early medieval times. 2. The Cholas sent an expedition against the Sailendra empire of South-East Asia and conquered some of the areas. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The Cholas were successful in defeating the Pandya and Chera rulers, establishing a stronghold in peninsular India, and significantly expanding their territorial control in South India during the early medieval period. For example, under Rajaraja I and Rajendra I, the Cholas defeated these rulers and extended their empire across peninsular India. Statement 2 is correct: The Cholas were also known for their naval power and maritime trade, which led them to conduct expeditions to South-East Asia. Their naval campaigns extended the Chola influence to regions like Sri Lanka, the Maldives, and Indonesia, where they challenged the Sailendra Empire. For instance, Rajendra Chola I led a successful naval expedition (1025 CE) against the Srivijaya Empire (modern-day Indonesia, Malaysia, and Thailand). The Cholas temporarily conquered some areas, demonstrating their naval supremacy in the region.

subject: Art and Culture
stem: Mrichchhakatika an ancient Indian book written by Shudraka deals with:
options: the love affair of a rich merchant with the daughter of a courtesan
the victory of Chandragupta II over the Shaka Kshatrapas of western India
the military expeditions and exploits of Samudragupta
the love affairs between a Gupta king and a princess of Kamarupa 146

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: the victory of Chandragupta II over the Shaka Kshatrapas of western India
solution.detail:
A is matched with 3: Varahamihira authored Brihat- Samhita, a significant text in ancient Indian astronomy and astrology, reflecting the scientific knowledge of the Gupta period. He also wrote Panchasiddhantika. B is matched with 4: Vishakhadatta is the author of two major historical Sanskrit dramas:
- Devi-Chandraguptam play which describes the love story of King Chandragupta II (Vikramaditya) and Queen Dhruvadevi.
- Mudrarakshasa is about Chandragupta Maurya’s rise with the help of Chanakya. C is matched with 2: Sudraka is known for Mrichchhakatikam (The Little Clay Cart), a Sanskrit play that tells the story of the romance between Charudatta, a noble but impoverished merchant, and Vasantika, a courtesan. The central themes include love, sacrifice, and the struggles of social classes, with an emphasis on the tensions between the upper and lower classes. D is matched with 5: Bilhana authored Vikramankadeva- Charita, an epic poem which celebrates the achievements and virtues of King Vikramaditya VI, who ruled the Western Chalukya dynasty (also known as the Kalyani Chalukyas) from 1076 to 1126 CE.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: East Timor was British colony for four centuries
The European colonial rulers handed over East Timor to Indonesia in 1975
The United Nations took over East Timor in 1999 to prepare it for independence
East Timor finally declared its independence in December 2001

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: The United Nations took over East Timor in 1999 to prepare it for independence
solution.detail:
East Timor (Timor-Leste), a Southeast Asian nation with Dili as its capital, was a Portuguese colony for over 400 years before Indonesia occupied it in 1975. Following a UN-sponsored referendum in 1999, where the majority voted for independence, violent clashes erupted. The UN Transitional Administration (UNTAET) took control to restore peace and oversee governance, leading to East Timor’s full independence in 2002. Portugal did not “hand over” the territory to Indonesia in 1975 - instead, Indonesia invaded and annexed East Timor following a power vacuum created by the impending withdrawal of Portugal, leading to a civil war within East Timor between political factions.
- East Timor declared its independence on May 20, 2002.

stem: Which among the following countries was the earliest to give women the right to vote?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: New Zealand
solution.detail: New Zealand was the first country in the world to grant women the right to vote in 1893. The USA gave women full voting rights at the federal level in 1920 with the 19th Amendment. subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which one among the following countries has the lowest GDP per capita?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: India
solution.detail:
In 2003, among the given countries, India had the lowest GDP per capita. Here’s a comparison of estimated GDP per capita (in USD) around that time:
- China – Higher due to rapid industrialization and economic reforms.
- Indonesia – Had a stronger economy than India, despite the 1997 Asian financial crisis.
- Sri Lanka – Had a smaller but more stable economy with a relatively higher GDP per capita than India.
- India – Despite economic growth, per capita income remained the lowest among these countries due to its large population and slower per capita income growth.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: The Basque people who are frequently in the news for their separatist activity live in:

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Spain
solution.detail: The Basque people primarily inhabit the Basque Country, which is located in northern Spain and southwestern France. However, their separatist movement has been more prominent in Spain, where the group ETA (Euskadi Ta Askatasuna) historically fought for an independent Basque state. Though ETA officially disbanded in 2018, Basque nationalism remains an important political issue in Spain.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: As per the Human Development Index given by UNDP, which one of the following sequences of South Asian countries is correct, in the order of higher to lower development?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Maldives–Sri Lanka–India–Pakistan
solution.detail: The Human Development Report 2003 by UNDP ranked South Asian countries based on the Human Development Index (HDI). Maldives led the region with strong health, literacy, and income levels, followed by Sri Lanka, which excelled in education and healthcare but lagged slightly in economic growth. India showed steady progress in literacy and life expectancy but faced challenges due to its large population and income inequality. Pakistan ranked the lowest among these nations, struggling with lower literacy rates, weaker healthcare, and slower economic growth.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: recommendations for tax reforms
solution.detail: The Kelkar Committee, led by Dr. Vijay L. Kelkar, recommended tax reforms to simplify India’s tax system. It proposed reducing tax rates, eliminating exemptions, broadening the tax base, and improving tax administration. The committee also advocated for GST-like reforms and lower customs and excise duties.

stem: With reference to Indian defence, which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: With the induction of Prithvi-II, the IAF is the only air force in the world with surface to surface ballistic missiles under its command

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Trishul is a supersonic surface to air missile with a range of 30 km
solution.detail:
Trishul, a short-range surface-to-air missile under India’s IGMDP, had a 9 km range, not 30 km. It was supersonic and later discontinued in 2008. Option (a), (b) and (d) are correct:
- Prithvi-II is a short-range surface-to-surface ballistic missile developed by DRDO. It was inducted into the Indian Air Force, making it the only air force in the world to operate ballistic missiles at the time.
- The Sukhoi Su-30MKI, a multi-role fighter aircraft of the IAF, is capable of launching precision-guided air-to-air and airto-surface missiles like BrahMos, Astra, and Kh-59.
- INS Prabal is a missile boat of the Indian Navy, capable of launching sea-to-sea missiles like SS-N-25 ‘Switchblade’ anti-ship missiles.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: The thermal power plant of Bokaro is located in:

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Jharkhand
solution.detail: The Bokaro Thermal Power Plant is located in Bokaro district, Jharkhand. It is one of India’s important coal-based power plants, operated by Damodar Valley Corporation (DVC) and NTPC. It is a coal-fired power plant, utilizing coal from nearby Jharia and Bokaro coalfields. It plays a crucial role in supplying electricity to Jharkhand, Bihar, West Bengal, and Odisha.

subject: Current Affairs
stem: Which one among the following has the largest shipyard in India?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Kochi
solution.detail: Cochin Shipyard Limited (CSL), located in Kochi, Kerala. It was established in 1972 and is India’s largest shipbuilding and maintenance facility. It builds large commercial ships, naval vessels, and aircraft carriers like INS Vikrant (India’s first indigenous aircraft carrier). CSL, the first Greenfield and most modern shipbuilding yard in India, has an enviable reputation for building high quality ships.

stem: With reference to the Indian economy, consider the following activities: 1. Agriculture, Forestry and Fishing 2. Manufacturing 3. Trade, Hotels, Transport and Communication 4. Financing, Insurance, Real Estate and Business services The decreasing order of the contribution of these sectors to the Gross Domestic Product (GDP) at factor cost at constant prices (2000-01) is :

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 3, 2, 4
solution.detail: In the fiscal year 2000-01, India’s GDP at factor cost (constant prices) was approximately ₹11,93,922 crore. The sectoral contributions were Agriculture, Forestry, and Fishing (24.7%), Trade, Hotels, Transport, and Communication (18.9%), Manufacturing (15.7%), and Financing, Insurance, Real Estate, and Business Services (13.1%), reflecting the dominance of agriculture and services in the economy during that period.
solution.extraEdge: According to the Provisional Estimates of Annual GDP for 2023-24 released by the Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation in May 2024, India’s Gross Value Added (GVA) at constant prices was primarily driven by the services sector, which contributed 52.1% to the total GVA. The industrial sector accounted for 29.6%, with manufacturing alone contributing 17.4%. Agriculture, forestry, and fishing contributed 18.3%.

stem: Which one of the following statements is correct with reference to FEMA in India?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Under FEMA, violation of foreign exchange rules has ceased to be a criminal offence
solution.detail: FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act) was enacted in 1999 and came into force in 2000. It replaced FERA (Foreign Exchange Regulation Act), which was more stringent and focused on criminalizing violations of foreign exchange laws. subject: Economy
stem: Which one of the following committees recomm- ended the abolition of reservation of items for the small scale sector in industry?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Abid Hussian Committee
solution.detail:
Option (a) is correct: The Abid Hussain Committee (1997) recommended the abolition of the reservation of items for the small-scale sector in industry. This recommendation was made to enhance the competitiveness of small-scale industries (SSIs) by opening up reserved items to medium and large-scale industries and promoting efficiency and modernization. Options (b) is incorrect: Narasimham Committee (1991 & 1998) focused on banking sector reforms to enhance financial stability and improve banking efficiency. Some of its recommendations include:
- Reducing Cash Reserve Ratio (CRR) and Statutory Liquidity Ratio (SLR).
- Recommended the establishment of Asset Reconstruction Companies (ARCs) to address Non-Performing Assets (NPAs).
- Proposed the autonomy of public sector banks and the establishment of a stronger regulatory framework under RBI. Options (c) is incorrect: Nayak Committee (1992) focused on improving credit availability for small-scale industries (SSIs). Key Recommendations:
- Banks should provide working capital equivalent to 20% of the annual turnover of SSIs.
- Simplify loan procedures and reduce collateral requirements for SSIs.
- Establish specialized branches for SSI lending.
- Recommended the formation of a dedicated financial institution to cater to small-scale industries. Options (d) is incorrect: Rakesh Mohan Committee (2004) analyzed small savings schemes and suggested reforms to align them with market rates. It recommended rationalization of interest rates on schemes like National Savings Certificates (NSCs) and Post Office Deposits. It proposed that small savings schemes should no longer distort the financial market and advocated better financial literacy for savers.

subject: Economy
stem: Debenture holders of a company are its:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: creditors
solution.detail: Debenture holders are creditors of a company as they lend money to the company in exchange for a fixed interest rate over a specified period. Unlike equity shareholders, debenture holders do not have voting rights in company decisions.

stem: Among the following, which planet takes maximum time for one revolution around the Sun?

Planets farther from the Sun have larger orbits, resulting in longer revolution periods. Option (b) is correct: Jupiter takes the longest time among the given options to orbit the Sun, requiring approximately 4,333 days(11.86 Earth years). This is due to its significantly larger orbital path compared to the other planets listed. Hence, this is the correct answer. Venus has the shortest revolution period among the listed planets, taking approximately 224.7 Earth days(0.615 years) to orbit the Sun.

subject: Geography
stem: Which one among the following covers the highest percentage of forest area in the world ?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Temperate coniferous forests
solution.detail:
Option (a) is correct: Temperate coniferous forests dominate due to their vast distribution in the boreal or taiga zones, covering large parts of North America, Europe, and Asia. They thrive in cold climates and represent the largest biome by area, accounting for the highest percentage of forest cover worldwide. Options (b), (c), and (d) are incorrect:
- Temperate Deciduous Forests: Found in areas with distinct seasons, like eastern North America, Europe, and East Asia, but their global coverage is smaller than that of coniferous forests.
- Tropical Monsoon Forests: Found in regions with monsoon climates (seasonal rainfall) like parts of Asia and Africa, they cover a smaller area compared to temperate coniferous forests.
- Tropical Rain Forests: Found near the Equator, these forests are rich in biodiversity but are geographically restricted to regions like the Amazon Basin, Congo Basin, and Southeast Asia.

subject: Geography
stem: Consider the following statements : 1. 2. India is the original home of the cotton plant. India is the first country in the world to develop hybrid cotton varieties leading to increased production. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: The Indian subcontinent is indeed the original home of the cotton plant. The Neolithic Site of Mehrgarh near Bolan Pass in Balochistan region of Pakistan has the oldest evidence of cotton. Many Harappan sites provide evidence of cotton cultivation. Statement 2 is correct: The first commercial cotton hybrid was Hybrid-4 (H-4), developed by Indian scientist Chandrakant T. Patel in 1970. This hybrid was created by crossing the Gujarat-67 variety with the American variety. These hybrids offer advantages like higher yields, improved resistance to pests and diseases, and better adaptability to diverse climatic conditions.

stem: The Genetic Engineering Approval Committee, whose permission is required for cultivation of any genetically modified crop such as Bt Cotton in India, is under the Union Ministry of :

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Environment and Forests
solution.detail:
The Genetic Engineering Appraisal Committee (GEAC) is the regulatory body responsible for granting environmental release of genetically modified (GM) crops like Bt Cotton in India, while the final approval for commercial cultivation is decided by the central government.
- It functions under the Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC). It was established under the 1989 Rules, which were framed under the Environment Protection Act, 1986.
- It oversees the safety assessment of GM crops before their commercial release. Final approvals for cultivation are based on GEAC’s recommendations, but the central government makes the final decision after considering inputs from other stakeholders like the Indian Council of Agricultural Research (ICAR) and the Ministry of Agriculture.”

Longitude is a geographic coordinate that specifies the eastwest position of a point on the Earth’s surface. It is measured in degrees (°), ranging from 0° at the Prime Meridian (which passes through Greenwich, England) to 180° East and 180° West. It is measured in degrees (°), ranging from 0° at the Equator to 90° North at the North Pole and 90° South at the South Pole. Lines of latitude, also called parallels, run parallel to the Equator.

stem: Consider the following statements : 1. The longitude of Jabalpur’s location is between those of Indore and Bhopal. 2. The latitude of Aurangabad’s location is between those of Vadodara and Pune. 3. Bangalore is situated more southward than Chennai. Which of these statements is/are correct ?

Moderation Effect of coasts: The presence of a sea reduces the annual range of temperature due to the high specific heat capacity of water, which absorbs and releases heat more slowly than land. This moderating effect prevents extreme temperature variations, leading to smaller differences between summer and winter temperatures in coastal areas.
- Continentality: It refers to the influence of a location’s distance from the sea on its climate. Inland areas experience greater temperature extremes because land heats up and cools down faster than water, resulting in hotter summers, colder winters, and a larger annual temperature range compared to coastal regions. 

EXTRA EDGE
 The latitude of the Tropic of Cancer is the imaginary circle at approximately 23.5° N. It marks the northernmost point where the Sun appears directly overhead at noon. This occurs during the June solstice (around June 21), when the Northern Hemisphere is tilted toward the Sun. The Tropic of Cancer passes through 17 countries, including: Mexico, Bahamas, India, Saudi Arabia, UAE, China and Taiwan.

subject: Geography
stem: The waterfall ‘Victoria’ is associated with the river:

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Zambezi
solution.detail:
Option (d) is correct: Victoria Falls, known locally as “Mosi-oa-Tunya” or “The Smoke That Thunders,” is located on the Zambezi River. It straddles the border between Zambia and Zimbabwe. The Zambezi River is the fourth-longest river in Africa and flows through several countries, including Zambia, Zimbabwe, Angola, and Mozambique. Options (a), (b) and (c) are incorrect:
- The Amazon River, which is the largest river in the world by discharge, flows through South America, primarily through Brazil, but does not have Victoria Falls.
- The Missouri River is a major river in the United States, flowing through several states. It does not have any waterfalls as famous as Victoria Falls.
- The St. Lawrence River flows through Canada and the United States, connecting the Great Lakes to the Atlantic Ocean. While it has several rapids and is an important waterway for shipping, it is not associated with Victoria Falls.

subject: Geography
stem: Israel has common borders with :

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Lebanon, Syria, Jordan and Egypt
solution.detail: Israel shares its borders with four neighboring countries: Lebanon to the north, Syria to the northeast, Jordan to the east, and Egypt to the southwest. These borders have evolved significantly since Israel’s establishment in 1948, influenced by various geopolitical events and agreements.
solution.extraEdge:
Lebanon: The border with Lebanon is defined by the Blue Line, established by the United Nations in 2000 to confirm Israel’s withdrawal from southern Lebanon. This line closely approximates the Green Line set under the 1949 armistice agreement between Lebanon and Israel. The area east of the Hasbani River is considered part of Syria and included in the Golan Heights.
- Syria: The border with Syria includes the Golan Heights, a strategically significant region that Israel captured during the 1967 Six-Day War.
- Jordan: The border with Jordan was established following the 1994 peace treaty between the two nations. This agreement marked the first peace treaty between Israel and an Arab country, leading to the normalization of diplomatic and economic relations.
- Egypt: The border with Egypt was defined by the 1979 peace agreement, which resulted in Israel returning the Sinai Peninsula to Egypt and dismantling all settlements there. This agreement was the first peace treaty between Israel and an Arab country, leading to the normalization of diplomatic and economic relations.
- Uruguay: Located in the southeastern part of the continent, Uruguay has coastlines along both the Atlantic Ocean and the Rio de la Plata estuary, enhancing its maritime connectivity.

stem: How did Sultan Qutb-ud-din Aibak die?
options: He was treacherously stabbed to death by one of his ambitious nobles
He was killed in a battle with Taj-u-din Yildiz, the ruler of Ghazni who entered into a contest with him over the capture of Punjab
He sustained injuries while besieging the fortress of Kalinjar in Bundelkhand and succumbed to them later
He died after a fall from his horse while playing Chaugan

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: He died after a fall from his horse while playing Chaugan
solution.detail:
Qutb-ud-Din Aibak (1206-1210 AD) was the first Sultan of Delhi and the founder of the Mamluk dynasty. Aibak’s notable achievements include the construction of the Qutb Minar in Delhi, which is a symbol of Islamic architecture, and his efforts to consolidate control over northern India by defeating Rajput kingdoms and expanding his empire. Option (d) is correct: Qutb-ud-din Aibak died in 1210 CE from a fatal accident while playing Chaugan (a Persian-origin game similar to polo). He fell from his horse during the match in Lahore and succumbed to his injuries. His death was accidental and occurred during a leisure activity, not due to treachery or battle. Other important rulers of the Mamluk dynasty are:
- Shamsuddin Iltutmish 1210 - 1236 AD
- Raziyya Sultan - 1236 - 1240 AD
- Ghiyasuddin Balban - 1266 - 1287 AD

stem: How did the Mughal Emperor Jahandarshah’s reign come to an early end?
options: He was deposed by his Wazir
He died due to a slip while climbing down the steps
He was defeated by his nephew in a battle
He died of sickness due to drinking

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: He was defeated by his nephew in a battle
solution.detail: Jahandar Shah (1661-1713) was the 9th Mughal Emperor who reigned from 1712 to 1713 and he was the son of Bahadur Shah. After the death of Bahadur Shah, a new element entered Mughals’ political arena, in which nobles were ‘king makers,’ and rulers were only ‘puppets’ in their hands. Jahandar Shah was considered as the first Mughal India puppet ruler as he won the seat of throne with the support of Zulfiqar Khan, the most powerful noble of the time. Jahandar Shah’s reign came to an early end in January 1713. He was defeated in the battle at Samugarh (a city near Agra) by his nephew Farrukhsiyar on 10th January 1713 with the help of Sayyid Brothers. Farrukshiyar proclaimed himself the Mughal emperor after the battle.

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Kitab-I-Nauras (The Book of 9 Rasas), a collection of songs praising both Hindu deities and Muslim saints, was composed by Sultan Ibrahim Adil Shah II of Adil Shahi dynasty in the 16th century. The fifty-nine songs and seventeen couplets of the book are written in Dakhni Urdu. It also indicates the ragas in which they were to be sung. He was a dhrupad singer and the player of an instrument called tambour. Statement 2 is correct: Amir Khusrau, a renowned Sufi poet, musician, and scholar, is credited with the foundation of Qawwali music in India. Qawali is a devotional form of Islamic music deeply embedded in the mystical traditions of Sufism, and Amir Khusrau is often regarded as its spiritual and musical architect in the Indian subcontinent.

stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct?

Sawai Jai Singh of Amber had Euclid’s Elements of Geometry’ translated into Sanskrit.
Sultan Tipu of Mysore gave money for the construction of the idol of Goddess Sharda in the Shringeri temple.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Ali Mardan Khan introduced the system of revenue farming in Bengal.
solution.detail: Statement (a) is incorrect: Murshid Quli Khan not Ali Mardan khan introduced revenue farming to improve finances and prevent the English East India Company from collecting taxes. Statement (b) is correct: Ranjit Singh established modern foundries in Lahore to manufacture cannons with the help of French General Claude Auguste Court. Statement (c) is correct: Sawai Jai Singh, king of Amber and a scholar of mathematics and astronomy, translated Euclid’s Elements of Geometry into Sanskrit and established an observatory at Delhi, Jaipur, Ujjain, Mathura and Varanasi. Statement (d) is correct: The Maratha ransacked Shringeri temple and looted after which Tipu gave monetary support to the temple for the construction of the Idol of Sharda.

subject: Modern History
topic: The Advent Of The Europeans In India
tags: The Advent Of The Europeans In India
stem: With reference to the entry of European powers into India, which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: The English opened their first factory in South India at Masulipatam.
In Eastern India, the English Company opened its first factory in Orissa in 1633.
Under the leadership of Dupleix, the French occupied Madras in 1746.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: The Portuguese captured Goa in 1499.
solution.detail: The entry of European powers into India marked a pivotal era in Indian history, characterized by trade, colonial expansion, and rivalry among European nations. These powers established factories, forts, and settlements to dominate trade routes and resources in India. Statement (a) is incorrect: The Portuguese, led by Afonso de Albuquerque, captured Goa in 1510, not 1499. The capture of Goa marked the establishment of Portuguese colonial rule in India and made Goa their stronghold for over four centuries. Statement (b) is correct: The English East India Company opened its first factory in South India at Masulipatam (modernday Machilipatnam) in 1611. This marked the beginning of English trade in South India. Statement (c) is correct: The English established their first factory in Eastern India at Hariharpur, near Balasore in Orissa, in 1633. This was a strategic move to expand their trading operations in Eastern India. Statement (d) is correct: Under the leadership of Joseph François Dupleix, the French occupied Madras (modern-day Chennai) in 1746 during the First Carnatic War. However, Madras was later returned to the British by the Treaty of Aix-la-Chapelle in 1748.

stem: In India, among the following locations, the Dutch established their earliest factory at:

solution.detail: The Dutch established factories on the Coromandel coast, in Gujarat, Uttar Pradesh, Bengal and Bihar. Amongst the above, they opened their earliest factory at Pulicat in 1609. Their other principal factories in India were at Surat (1616), Bimlipatam (1641), Karaikal (1645), Chinsura (1653), Kasimbazar (near Murshidabad), Balasore, Patna, Nagapatam (1658) and Cochin (1663).

subject: Modern History
stem: Which one of the following provisions was not made in the Charter Act of 1833?
options: The trading activities of the East India Company were to be abolished.
The designation of the supreme authority was to be changed as the Governor-General of India in Council.
All law-making powers to be conferred on the Governor- General in Council.
An Indian was to be appointed as a Law Member in the Governor-General’s Council.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: The trading activities of the East India Company were to be abolished.
solution.detail:
Between 1784 and 1857, the British passed several acts to centralize control, regulate the East India Company’s administration, and address rising unrest in India. These measures ultimately led to the Indian Rebellion of 1857, marking the end of Company rule and the beginning of direct British governance. Option (a) is correct.
- The Charter Act of 1813 abolished the East India Company’s monopoly on trade with India, except for certain key areas such as the trade in tea and commerce with China.
- The Regulating Act of 1773 was a key piece of legislation that aimed to improve the administration of the British East India Company. It required the Company to submit correspondence and documents to the British government for scrutiny.
- The Government of India Act 1858 transferred the governance of India from the East India Company to the British Crown following the Indian Rebellion of 1857. The dual system introduced by the Pitt’s India Act came to an end. The Governor-general became the Viceroy.
- The Pitt’s India Act of 1784 established a dual system of government, where the East India Company was controlled by both a Board of Directors and a Board of Control, with the latter controlling its policy and finances.

subject: Modern History
stem: With reference to the Indian freedom struggle, which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: The Rowlatt Act aroused a wave of popular indignation and led to the Jallianwala Bagh massacre
Subhas Chandra Bose formed the Forward Bloc
Bhagat Singh was one of the founders of hindustan socialist republican association
In 1931, the Congress Session at Karachi opposed the Gandhi- Irwin Pact

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: In 1931, the Congress Session at Karachi opposed the Gandhi- Irwin Pact
solution.detail:
The Congress Session at Karachi (1931), under the leadership of Jawaharlal Nehru, did not oppose the Gandhi- Irwin Pact. It supported the pact, which was an agreement between Mahatma Gandhi and Lord Irwin (the Viceroy), aimed at ending the Civil Disobedience Movement in exchange for certain concessions, including the release of political prisoners and the right to make salt for personal use. Statements (a), (b) and (c) are correct:
- The Rowlatt Act (1919) led to widespread protests, which culminated in the Jallianwala Bagh massacre in April 1919.
- Subhas Chandra Bose formed the Forward Bloc in 1939 to unite all like minded left-wing factions of the Indian National Congress.
- Bhagat Singh was one of the key members of the Hindustan Socialist Republican Association (HSRA). Its members became symbols of sacrifice and resistance.

subject: Modern History
stem: When Congress leaders condemned the Montagu- Chelmsford Report, many moderates left the party to form the:

This organization aimed to support gradual constitutional reforms and believed in constitutional methods for achieving Indian selfrule.

subject: Modern History
stem: The leader of the Bardoli Satyagraha was:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel
solution.detail: The Bardoli Satyagraha was a significant peasant movement in Bardoli, Gujarat in 1928, led by Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel. It was sparked by the British government’s decision to increase land revenue by 22%, which severely affected the farmers. He organized the farmers to refuse to pay the increased taxes, leading to a successful non-violent protest that resulted in the reversal of the tax hike. His leadership earned him the title “Sardar” (leader) from the women of Bardoli for his success in forcing the British to roll back the revenue hike.

subject: Modern History
stem: An important aspect of the Cripps Mission of 1942 was:
options: that all Indian States should join the Indian Union as a condition to consider any degree of autonomy for India
the creation of an Indian Union with Dominion status very soon after the end of Second World War

the active participation and cooperation of the Indian people, communities and political parties in the British war efforts as a condition for granting independence with full sovereign status to India after the war
the framing of a constitution for the entire Indian Union, with no separate constitution for any province, and a Union Constitution to be accepted by all provinces

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: the creation of an Indian Union with Dominion status very soon after the end of Second World War
solution.detail: The Cripps Mission was sent to India in March 1942 by the British government to secure Indian support for the British war effort during World War II. The mission, led by Sir Stafford Cripps, aimed to offer India greater autonomy in return for cooperation. The Cripps Mission proposed the creation of an Indian Union with Dominion status after the war, which would grant India a degree of self-governance but still leave it under British sovereignty. The Cripps Mission was a failure because it did not meet the expectations of Indian political leaders and both Indian National Congress and Muslim League rejected the proposals.

stem: With reference to colonial rule in India, what was sought by the Ilbert Bill in 1883?
options: To bring Indians and Europeans at par as far as the criminal jurisdiction of courts was concerned.
To impose severe restrictions on the freedom of the native press as it was perceived to be hostile to colonial rulers.
To encourage the native Indians to appear for civil service examinations by conducting them in India.
To allow native Indians to possess arms by amending the Arms Act.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: To bring Indians and Europeans at par as far as the criminal jurisdiction of courts was concerned.
solution.detail: The Ilbert Bill was introduced in 1883 by Sir Courtenay Ilbert, the then Law Member of the Viceroy’s Council, to amend the criminal procedure code in British India. The bill sought to allow Indian judges, including the district magistrates, to try British offenders in criminal cases. At the time, only British judges had the authority to try European offenders. The proposal faced strong opposition from the European community in India, leading to protests. The British government eventually modified the bill, allowing Indian judges to try Europeans only if they consented. The controversy highlighted racial discrimination in colonial India.

subject: Modern History
stem: During the colonial period in India, what was the purpose of the Whitley Commission?
options: To review the fitness of India for further political reforms.
To report on existing conditions of labour and to make recommendations.
To draw up a plan for financial reforms for India.
To develop a comprehensive scheme for Civil Services in India.

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: To report on existing conditions of labour and to make recommendations.
solution.detail: The Whitley Commission (1929), also known as the Royal Commission on Labour in India, was established by the British colonial government to investigate labour conditions in India. Chaired by Sir William Whitley, its purpose was to examine issues like wages, working hours, and living conditions of industrial workers. It recommended improving labour welfare, establishing labour unions, and creating welfare funds, along with measures for industrial peace, better working conditions, and health and safety improvements for workers.

subject: Modern History
stem: In India, the first Bank of limited liability managed by Indians and founded in 1881 was:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Oudh Commercial Bank
solution.detail: The Oudh Commercial Bank, founded in 1881 in Faizabad, Uttar Pradesh, was the first Indian-managed bank to be established as a limited liability company. It was created by local businessmen and marked a significant shift in India’s banking sector, which had previously been dominated by the British. The bank’s establishment highlighted the increasing involvement of Indians in economic activities, especially in banking, and it aimed to provide financial services to local industries and trade, representing a milestone in Indiancontrolled commercial banking.

subject: Modern History
stem: The aim of education as stated by the Wood’s Despatch of 1854 was:
options: the creation of employment opportunities for native Indians
the spread of western culture in India
the promotion of literacy among the people using English medium
the introduction of scientific research and rationalism in the traditional Indian education

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: the introduction of scientific research and rationalism in the traditional Indian education
solution.detail: The Wood’s Despatch of 1854, often called the “Magna Carta of English Education in India,” laid the foundation for modern educational policies in colonial India. Its primary focus was to establish a comprehensive system of education, emphasizing scientific learning and rational thinking over traditional religious teachings. The dispatch included support for education for girls and encouragement for private and missionary involvement. It provided government grants to qualifying schools. In it’s three tier educational hierarchy of Education, vernacular languages for basic education, English and Indian languages at the secondary level and English at the Higher level i.e. at University level (Calcutta, Bombay, and Madras).

stem: Which of the following Constitutional Amendments are related to raising the number of Members of Lok Sabha to be elected from the States?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: 7th and 31st

subject: Polity
stem: The power to enlarge the jurisdiction of the Supreme Court of India with respect to any matter included in the Union List of Legislative Powers rests with:
options: The Union Ministry of Law, Justice and Company Affairs

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: The Parliament
solution.detail: According to Article 138(1) of the Constitution of India, Parliament has the power to confer additional jurisdiction on the Supreme Court in matters included in the Union List. This means that if the Parliament deems it necessary, it can pass a law to grant the Supreme Court the authority to hear and decide on specific matters that fall within the jurisdiction of the union list. Also under article 138(2) the Supreme Court shall have such further jurisdiction and powers with respect to any matter as the Government of India and the Government of any State may confer by special agreement.

subject: Polity

There is a constitutional provision for nominating two members belonging to the Anglo-Indian community to the Rajya Sabha.
There is no constitutional bar for a nominated member to be appointed as a Union minister.
A nominated member can vote both in the Presidential and Vice Presidential election. 200

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: There is no constitutional bar for a nominated member to be appointed as a Union minister.
solution.detail: Statement (a) is incorrect: Under article 80(1) (a), The President nominates 12 members to the Rajya Sabha from people who have special knowledge or practical experience in art, literature, science and social service. Before 2020 2 members were nominated from the Anglo-Indian community in Lok Sabha by the President. Statement (b) is incorrect: Before 2020, the President under article 331 nominated two members from the Anglo-Indian community to the Lok Sabha (and not Rajya Sabha) if the community was not adequately represented. This provision was abolished by the 104th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2019, which came into effect in January 2020. Statement (c) is correct: A nominated member of either Rajya Sabha or Lok Sabha has mostly the same rights and privileges as an elected member, including the right to be appointed as a Union minister. However, nominated members cannot vote in Presidential elections. Statement (d) is incorrect: Under article 55, only elected members of both Houses of Parliament and the elected members of the Legislative Assemblies of States and Union Territories participate in the Presidential election.Therefore nominated members cannot vote in the Presidential election. However, both elected and nominated members can vote in Vice Presidential elections.

subject: Polity
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The joint sitting of the two houses of the Parliament in India is sanctioned under Article 108 of the Constitution. 2. The first joint sitting of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha was held in the year 1961. 3. The second joint sitting of the two Houses of Indian Parliament was held to pass the Banking Service Commission (Repeal) Bill. Which of these statements is correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2 and 3
solution.detail:
Joint sitting is an extraordinary machinery provided by the constitution to resolve a deadlock between the two houses over the passage of a bill. Statement 1 is correct: Article 108 of the Indian Constitution provides for a joint sitting of both Houses of Parliament if after a bill is passed by one House and transmitted to the other House –
- The other House rejects this bill, or
- The Houses do not agree on the amendments made to the bill, or
- More than six months elapse with the bill being received by the other House without it being passed. Statement 2 is correct: The First Joint sitting was held in May, 1961 in which Dowry Prohibition Bill 1960 was passed Statement 3 is correct: The second joint sitting of the two Houses of the Indian Parliament was held to pass the Banking Service Commission (Repeal) Bill. The Bill had been passed by the Lok Sabha but was rejected by the Rajya Sabha. As a result, a joint sitting was convened to resolve the deadlock, and the Bill was passed.

subject: Polity
stem: Which one of the following Bills must be passed by each House of the Indian Parliamentary separately by special majority?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Constitution Amendment Bill
solution.detail: According to Article 368 of the Indian Constitution, an amendment to the Constitution can be initiated only by introducing a Bill in either House of Parliament. The Bill must be passed in each House by a majority of the total membership of that House and by a two-thirds majority of the members present and voting (Special Majority). Once both Houses pass the Bill, it is presented to the President, who must give assent. Upon the President’s assent, the Constitution stands amended in accordance with the terms of the Bill.

stem: Consider the following statements: 1. India launched its first full-fledged meteorological satellite (METSAT) in September, 2002 2. For the first time, the space vehicle PSLV C-4 carried a payload of more than 1000 kg into a geosynchronous orbit Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: METSAT (renamed as Kalpana - 1) was the first in the series of exclusive meteorological satellites built by ISRO. Statement 1 is correct: India successfully launched its first full-fledged meteorological satellite, METSAT using the PSLV-C4 rocket. It was India’s first dedicated meteorological satellite, designed to monitor weather conditions and cyclone development in the Indian Ocean region. This demonstrated ISRO’s ability to deploy heavier payloads into higher orbits, advancing India’s space technology capabilities.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following statements: A 4-wheel vehicle moving in a sharp circular path at high speed will: 1. Overturn about its outer wheels 2. Overturn about its inner wheels 3. Skid outwards 4. Skid inwards Which of these statements are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: When a vehicle takes a sharp turn at high speed, the centrifugal force may push the vehicle outwards. This can cause the vehicle to tilt outwards and potentially overturn about its outer wheels. Statement 2 is incorrect: This is less likely to happen. The centrifugal force pushes the vehicle outwards, not inwards. Statement 3 is correct: If the friction between the tires and the road is insufficient to provide the necessary centripetal force, the vehicle may skid outwards, away from the center of the turn. Statement 4 is incorrect: Skidding typically occurs outwards, not inwards, due to the centrifugal force.

stem: An oil tanker partially filled with oil moves forward on a level road with uniform acceleration. The free surface of oil then:
options: is inclined to the horizontal with smaller depth at the rear end
is inclined to the horizontal with larger depth at the rear end

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: is inclined to the horizontal with larger depth at the rear end
solution.detail: When the oil tanker accelerates forward, the oil inside experiences an inertial force (pseudo force) acting in the opposite direction to the acceleration. This causes the free surface of the oil to tilt.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Steam at 100°C and boiling water at 100°C contain the same amount of heat. 2. Latent heat of fusion of ice is equal to the latent heat of vaporization of water. 3. In an air-conditioner, heat is extracted from the room air at the evaporator coils and is rejected out at the condenser coils. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Only 3
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: Steam at 100°C contains significantly more heat than boiling water at 100°C. This is because steam has absorbed additional energy in the form of latent heat of vaporization which is the energy required to convert water at 100°C into steam at the same temperature without changing its temperature. Statement 2 is incorrect: The latent heat of fusion of ice (the energy required to convert ice at 0°C to water at 0°C) is approximately 334 kJ/kg while the latent heat of vaporization of water (the energy required to convert water at 100°C to steam at 100°C) is approximately 2260 kJ/kg. Statement 3 is correct: In an air-conditioner the refrigerant absorbs heat from the room air at the evaporator coils and cools the air. This heat is then transported to the condenser coils, where it is released to the outside environment. This process is based on the principles of thermodynamics and the refrigeration cycle.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Diffusion of light in the atmosphere takes place due to:

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: dust particles
solution.detail: The diffusion of light in the atmosphere occurs mainly due to dust particles and aerosols, which scatter sunlight in all directions. This scattering, known as Mie scattering, happens when particles like dust or water droplets are comparable in size to the wavelength of light, scattering all wavelengths almost equally. It causes the sky to appear uniformly bright and ensures visibility even in shadows. Other aerosols like Water Vapors, Smoke, etc can also contribute to the diffusion effect. However, dust particles are the most prominent cause in this context.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: The velocity of sound in air increases with the increase of temperature.
The velocity of sound in air is independent of pressure.
The velocity of sound in air decreases as the humidity increases.
The velocity of sound in air is not affected by the change in amplitude and frequency.

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: The velocity of sound in air decreases as the humidity increases.
solution.detail: Option (c) is incorrect: Humidity (water vapor) actually increases the speed of sound in air. This is because water molecules are lighter than nitrogen and oxygen molecules, reducing the overall density of the air. The speed of sound in humid air is higher than in dry air at the same temperature. Options (a), (b) and (d) are correct: The velocity of sound in air increases with temperature because higher temperatures increase the energy of air molecules, allowing sound to travel faster. The velocity of sound in air is independent of pressure at constant temperature since both pressure and density change proportionally, leaving the speed unchanged. The velocity of sound is unaffected by amplitude and frequency as it depends only on the medium›s properties like temperature, density, and composition.

stem: With reference to ionic compounds, consider the following statements: 1. Ionic compounds are insoluble in alcohol. 2. Ionic compounds in the solid state are good conductors of electricity. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Only 1
solution.detail: Ionic compounds are chemical compounds composed of positively and negatively charged ions held together by strong electrostatic forces of attraction. Statement 1 is correct: Ionic compounds generally dissolve in water due to its high polarity, but alcohols are less polar compared to water, which makes most ionic compounds insoluble in alcohol. Statement 2 is incorrect: In the solid state, the ions in ionic compounds are fixed in a rigid lattice structure and cannot move freely, which prevents the conduction of electricity.

stem: Regarding the atom of a chemical element , the magnetic quantum number refers to:

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Orientation
solution.detail: Option (a) is correct: The magnetic quantum number of an element specifies the orientation of an orbital in space relative to an external magnetic field. It determines the number of orbitals and their spatial orientation within a given subshell (defined by the azimuthal quantum number, l). Options (b), (c) and (d) are incorrect: Shape is determined by the azimuthal quantum number; Size is determined by the principal quantum number; Spin is determined by the spin quantum number, which specifies the spin orientation of the electron.

stem: Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: Liquid sodium is employed as a coolant in nuclear reactors.
Calcium carbonate is an ingredient of tooth paste.
Bordeaux mixture consists of sodium sulphate and lime.
Zinc amalgams are used as a dental filling.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Liquid sodium is employed as a coolant in nuclear reactors.
solution.detail:
Option (a) is correct: Liquid sodium is used as a coolant in certain types of nuclear reactors, particularly fast breeder reactors. Sodium is an effective coolant because of its high thermal conductivity and low neutron absorption properties, which are ideal for cooling the reactor core. Options (b), (c) and (d) are incorrect:
- Dental amalgams are typically made of silver, mercury, and other metals. Calcium carbonate is not a must-include ingredient in toothpastes. Though it is sometimes used in toothpaste as an abrasive that helps remove plaque, stains, and debris from teeth.
- Bordeaux mixture is made from copper sulfate and lime (calcium hydroxide). It is used as a fungicide and pesticide in agriculture to prevent fungal diseases like downy mildew in crops.
- Zinc amalgam is not commonly used in dental fillings, although zinc oxide is sometimes used in dental cements and temporary fillings.

stem: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: The presence of NaCl increases the rate of setting of plaster of Paris.
Gypsum is added to the cement to slow down its rate of setting.
All alkaline earth metals form hydrated salts.
Barium and strontium are found free in nature.

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: Barium and strontium are found free in nature.
solution.detail: Option (a) is correct: The presence of sodium chloride (NaCl) can speed up the setting of Plaster of Paris by increasing ionic concentration, altering solubility, and facilitating crystal growth. Option (b) is correct: Gypsum (CaSO₄·2H₂O) is added to cement to slow down its rate of setting and prevent it from hardening too quickly. Gypsum retards the rapid hydration of C₃A in cement, controlling the setting time. Option (c) is correct: Alkaline earth metals (group 2 elements) typically form hydrated salts, especially the heavier ones like calcium, strontium, and barium. These metals readily form salts like MgCl₂·6H₂O that can absorb water molecules and become hydrated. Option (d) is incorrect: Barium and strontium are highly reactive metals and do not occur freely in nature. They are typically found in mineral compounds such as barium sulfate (barite) and strontium carbonate. Directions: The following Items consist of two statements. subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following is produced during the formation of photochemical smog?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Ozone
solution.detail:
Photochemical smog is a type of air pollution that occurs when sunlight reacts with pollutants like nitrogen oxides and volatile organic compounds (VOCs), including hydrocarbons. The reaction leads to the formation of ozone, which is a key component of photochemical smog. Key Components of Photochemical Smog are:
- Nitrogen Oxides – Released from vehicles, industrial emissions, and power plants. They react with sunlight to form ozone.
- Hydrocarbons: Organic compounds that also contribute to ozone formation when exposed to sunlight.
- Ozone: The primary harmful product of photochemical smog, which can cause respiratory problems and damage to vegetation.
- Methane is a potent greenhouse gas, but it does not directly form photochemical smog. While it can contribute indirectly to smog formation by reacting with other pollutants, it is not a primary component like ozone, hydrocarbons, or nitrogen oxides.

stem: Consider the following statements: 1. The common blue green algae, Spirogyra and Ulothrix, are found in both freshwater ponds and oceans. 2. The Chameleon can look ahead with one eye, and at the same time look behind with another. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Spirogyra and Ulothrix are types of blue green algae that can be found in both freshwater and marine environments. They are typically found in ponds, rivers, and even oceans in some cases. Spirogyra is known for its spiral-shaped chloroplasts and forms slimy green filaments/ mats on the water surface, especially when nutrients like nitrogen and phosphorus are abundant while Ulothrix is characterized by its simple, unbranched filaments and typically grows in both freshwater ponds and coastal regions. These algae play an important role in the aquatic food chain. Statement 2 is correct: Chameleons have a unique ability to move their eyes independently of each other. They can focus one eye on an object in front while the other eye looks in a different direction, even behind them. This independent eye movement enables them to have a 360-degree view of their surroundings, aiding in predator detection and prey surveillance. 386. stem: Consider the following statements: 1. Molasses is a by-product of the sugar production process. 2. Bagasse obtained in the sugar mills is used as a fuel in the boilers to generate steam for the sugar factories. 3. Sugar can only be produced from sugarcane as the raw material. Which of these statements are correct?

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: Molasses is a thick, dark syrup that remains after the extraction of sugar from sugarcane or sugar beets. It is a common by-product in the sugar industry. It serves as a feedstock for producing synthetic chemical fertilizers and ethanol. Statement 2 is correct: Bagasse, the fibrous residue left after extracting juice from sugarcane, is commonly used as a biofuel in the sugar industry to generate steam and electricity, making the process more energy-efficient. Statement 3 is incorrect: Sugar is produced from various raw materials including sugarcane and sugar beets. Other sources for sugar production include sorghum and palm sap.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Consider the following conditions of a sick human body 1. Swollen lymph nodes 2. Sweating at night 3. Loss of memory 4. Loss of weight Which of these are symptoms of AIDS?

solution.verdict: Option D is correct: 1, 2, 3 and 4
solution.detail: The word AIDS stands for Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome. This means deficiency of the immune system, acquired during the lifetime of an individual. ‘Syndrome’ means a group of symptoms. AIDS is caused by the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV), a member of a group of viruses called retrovirus. It leads to various symptoms such as Swollen lymph nodes occur due to immune system deterioration, commonly affecting the neck, armpits, and groin. Night sweats and recurring fevers are signs of opportunistic infections as the body struggles to regulate temperature. Memory loss and cognitive impairment arise when the virus affects the nervous system, leading to neurological complications. Significant weight loss results from chronic infections, metabolic changes, and increased energy demands.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: With reference to the work of human kidney, consider the following statements 1. After the waste is removed in the kidney, the clean blood is sent back through renal artery. 2. From Bowman’s capsule, the filtered liquid passes through tiny tubes where much of the glucose is reabsorbed and sent back to the blood in the renal vein. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option B is correct: Only 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is incorrect: The renal artery is responsible for delivering oxygenated blood from the heart to the kidneys for filtration. After the kidneys filter the blood by removing waste products and excess substances, the cleansed blood is returned to the systemic circulation via the renal vein, not the renal artery. Statement 2 is correct: From Bowman’s capsule, the glomerular filtrate(filtered liquid) passes into the proximal convoluted tubule (PCT), where essential substances, including glucose, are actively reabsorbed. This reabsorbed glucose along with other nutrients is then transported into the peritubular capillaries and eventually returned to the bloodstream via the renal vein. This ensures that the body retains necessary nutrients while excreting waste.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
options: Milk contains none of the B-vitamins.
Vitamin-A (retinol) deficiency leads to dry and scaly skin.
One of the symptoms of scurvy is pain in the joints.
Vitamin B1 (thiamine) deficiency can lead to heart failure.

solution.verdict: Option A is correct: Milk contains none of the B-vitamins.
solution.detail: Milk does contain several B-vitamins such as Vitamin B2 (riboflavin) and Vitamin B12 (cobalamin) along with Vitamin D, calcium, and proteins. For instance, a 200ml glass of semi-skimmed milk provides approximately 0.47 mg of riboflavin, which is about 33% of the recommended daily intake. Options (b), (c) and (d) are correct: Vitamin A is found in foods like carrots, liver, and dairy products and is crucial for maintaining healthy skin. A deficiency in vitamin A can cause xerosis (dry, scaly skin) due to its role in maintaining healthy skin and mucous membranes. Scurvy is caused by a deficiency in vitamin C. One of its symptoms is joint pain, along with others like fatigue, swollen gums, and bruising. Vitamin C is found in citrus fruits, amla, and guava. A severe deficiency in Vitamin B1(thiamine) can result in beriberi, which affects the cardiovascular system, potentially leading to heart failure. Symptoms include shortness of breath, swelling of the lower legs, and increased heart rate. It is present in whole grains, nuts, and legumes.

subject: Science and Tech
stem: With reference to normal human beings, consider the following statements: 1. In response to the presence of HCl, secretin is produced from the duodenum. 2. Enterogastrone is produced in the small intestine in response to the presence of fatty acids. Which of these statements is/are correct?

solution.verdict: Option C is correct: Both 1 and 2
solution.detail: Statement 1 is correct: When hydrochloric acid (HCl) from the stomach enters the duodenum, it lowers the pH of intestinal contents. This triggers S-cells in the duodenal lining to release secretin. Secretin signals the pancreas to release bicarbonate-rich fluid. This fluid neutralizes the acid, creating an optimal environment for digestive enzymes to function. Statement 2 is correct: Enterogastrone refers to a group of hormones secreted by the duodenal mucosa when dietary fats are present. These hormones, including secretin and cholecystokinin (CCK) slows down gastric motility and inhibit stomach secretions. This regulation ensures that chyme moves gradually from the stomach to the small intestine, promoting better fat digestion and absorption.
